summary: Selected one bounded runtime interop safety improvement: escape and validate foreign interop names and metadata before JavaScript source emission and native text IR emission.

javascript: src/Compiler/Emit/JavaScript.clasp:874-1043 emits foreign/package interop code and manifests by raw concatenation of `foreignDeclName`, `foreignDeclRuntimeName`, package specifier, declaration path, and signature.
src/Compiler/Ast.clasp:1410-1441 parses quoted foreign strings and unescapes `\"`, `\n`, `\r`, and `\t`, so the emitter must re-escape those values when embedding them in JavaScript.
native: src/Compiler/Emit/Native.clasp:64-69 renders native text IR string literals as `string("... ")` by raw concatenation.

src/Compiler/Emit/Native.clasp:623-629 emits JSON runtime binding metadata safely via `jsonFieldText`, but native text runtime binding metadata still concatenates foreign names and runtime names directly.
src/Compiler/Emit/NativeJson.clasp:61-153 already emits lowered native expressions as JSON with `jsonFieldText` and is the safer reference path for string-bearing data.
bootstrap: clasp-compiler.cabal references `deprecated/bootstrap/src`, but that directory is absent in this checkout.

If the bootstrap Haskell source tree is restored, mirror the same foreign interop escaping and identifier validation in `deprecated/bootstrap/src/Clasp/Emit/JavaScript.hs` and any native/bootstrap emitter that serializes foreign metadata.
tests: scripts/test-js-process-runtime.sh already performs focused JavaScript emitter/runtime interop checks and is the best place for a fast regression fixture.
scripts/test-native-claspc.sh already covers native runtime bindings and should receive a small native-image regression when implementation touches native text/image emission.
test/Main.hs references bootstrap compiler APIs, but the corresponding bootstrap source tree is not present in this workspace.

bounded_improvement.title: Foreign interop emission must fail closed for unsafe identifiers and must encode every string-valued metadata field.
bounded_improvement.risk: AI-generated or user-provided `foreign` declarations can currently place parsed quoted text directly into emitted JavaScript source, import declarations, manifest object literals, package binding maps, and native text IR. Ordinary `LowerLiteral` JavaScript strings are safe because they use `encode`; the foreign/package interop path does not consistently use that escaping boundary.
exact_files_to_change: src/Compiler/Emit/JavaScript.clasp: add small helpers around `emitJsString` for JS string literal fields and use them in `emitForeignSourceLiteral`, `emitForeignDeclarationLiteral`, `emitHostBindingEntry`, `emitPackageImportEntry`, `emitPackageBindingEntry`, and `emitForeignBindings`.

src/Compiler/Emit/JavaScript.clasp: add a `jsIdentifierIsSafe`/`emitJsIdentifier` gate for values emitted in identifier position, especially `foreignDeclRuntimeName` in `emitPackageForeignImport` and `foreignDeclName` in `packageBindingIdentifier` and exported foreign wrappers.

src/Compiler/Checker.clasp or src/Compiler/Ast.clasp: surface a compile/check diagnostic for package foreign declarations whose imported runtime name is not a safe JavaScript import binding identifier. Prefer checker diagnostics if the implementation can report them without widening the parser.

src/Compiler/Emit/Native.clasp: either escape native text IR string payloads with a dedicated `emitNativeStringLiteral` helper or route string-bearing native consumption through the existing JSON image path; at minimum cover `LowerLiteral` and `emitNativeRuntimeBindingText`.
src/embedded.primary.clasp and promoted native/source cache artifacts: regenerate only if the self-hosted compiler promotion flow requires it after source changes.
expected_behavior: Valid existing foreign declarations with normal identifiers and package specifiers keep the same runtime behavior.
Quoted package specifiers, declaration paths, signatures, foreign names, and runtime names that appear in JS string/object positions are emitted using JSON-compatible string escaping.

Package-import runtime names that would be emitted in JavaScript identifier/import-binding position are rejected before emission with a deterministic diagnostic instead of producing malformed or injected JavaScript.
Native JSON images remain semantically unchanged, while native text IR no longer lets string contents or foreign runtime names introduce native IR delimiters.

expected_tests: Update scripts/test-js-process-runtime.sh or add scripts/test-js-foreign-interop-safety.sh with a generated Clasp fixture containing a package foreign declaration whose specifier/declaration/signature include escaped quotes and newline characters; compile to JS and assert the emitted module remains syntactically valid with `node --check` and contains encoded literals rather than raw payload text.

Add a negative JS/package foreign fixture with an unsafe imported runtime name such as a name containing a quote, brace, comma, or semicolon; `claspc --json check` or `claspc --json compile` should fail with the new deterministic diagnostic and should not write a malformed JS module.

Update scripts/test-native-claspc.sh with a native-image compile fixture for a runtime foreign declaration whose runtime name includes quote/newline-like data if such names stay allowed for runtime-map interop; parse the emitted native image JSON and assert the binding string round-trips without corrupting adjacent fields.
If `deprecated/bootstrap/src` is restored, add matching Haskell tests in test/Main.hs for bootstrap JS emission of escaped foreign package metadata and unsafe package import name rejection.
compatibility_notes: This is a fail-closed trust-boundary change for package imports: valid JavaScript import binding names remain compatible, while names that cannot be represented safely as import bindings become diagnostics.

Runtime foreign declarations that are resolved through `globalThis.__claspRuntime` can continue supporting broader string runtime names if emitted only through encoded manifest strings and map lookups.
Using `encode`/JSON-style string emission should preserve visible output for normal ASCII package names and paths except for harmless escape normalization.
Native JSON image consumers should not see schema changes; native text IR consumers may see escaped string spelling but should receive the same decoded values after parsing.
verification_commands_for_implementation: timeout 120 env -u CLASP_CLASPC -u CLASPC_BIN CLASP_PROJECT_ROOT="$PWD" bash scripts/test-js-process-runtime.sh
timeout 180 env -u CLASP_CLASPC -u CLASPC_BIN CLASP_PROJECT_ROOT="$PWD" bash scripts/test-native-claspc.sh
commands_run_in_audit: Inspected src/Compiler/Emit/JavaScript.clasp, src/Compiler/Emit/Native.clasp, src/Compiler/Emit/NativeJson.clasp, src/Compiler/Ast.clasp, clasp-compiler.cabal, test/Main.hs, and scripts/test-js-process-runtime.sh.
rg/sed/nl static inspection of foreign, package import, runtime binding, and native emission surfaces.
commands_not_run: bash scripts/verify-all.sh was intentionally not run per explicit task instruction.
Broad claspc check/run harnesses were not run because this audit did not implement behavior changes.
residual_risks: The absent deprecated/bootstrap source tree prevents direct bootstrap emitter inspection in this workspace.
The follow-up implementation must decide whether unsafe foreign interop diagnostics belong in parser/checker or as an emitter error path exposed by the CLI.
Native text IR escaping depends on the runtime/native parser expectations; prefer a fixture that parses the resulting native image rather than a string-only grep.
